D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Foxfire — Mycena Glows in the Dark Forest

around 80 mushroom species glow continuously through luciferase/luciferin chemistry; Mycena chlorophos famously lights up the Japanese forest floor at 530 nm green

Fungi were the longest-mysterious bioluminescent kingdom — the chromophore was only fully identified in 2017 (Kotlobay). It uses 3-hydroxyhispidin + luciferase; emits steady 530 nm green light from the mycelium and fruiting body. Brightest in Mycena chlorophos, Neonothopanus gardneri. The signal probably attracts spore-dispersing arthropods.

Drag time of night — fungal glow stays roughly constant unlike firefly flashes. Toggle species changes brightness peak.
